---
title: "Home for Incurable and Infirm Women, The"
---

## Home for Incurable and Infirm Women, The

**Type:** organization

### About

Mentioned 26 April 1864.  This was run by [[person:1427]Louisa Twining], and operated from 21 Great Ormond Street.  Twining described its work in an article in <i>MP</i> (April 1864) 437-449;  and further in a letter <i>MP</i> (August 1869) 205-6.  The latter gives the number of inmates as 27.


Some correspondence of the Charity Organization Society with an outfit of this name, running a home for respectable women in reduced circumstances at Whetstone, is held by the London Metropolitan Archives.

### Letters Mentioning This Organization


---

[← Back to all letters](../letters.html)
